methods.description: This method involves the statistical analysis and integration of trace metal, physicochemical, bacterial 16S sequencing, and metabolomics data to assess baseline conditions of estuaries and identify relationships among datasets.

steps.description: The trace metal and physicochemical data were used to assess the baseline condition of the sampled estuaries against the current Australian and New Zealand Guidelines for Fresh and Marine Water Quality in sediments.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess baseline conditions of estuaries using trace metal and physicochemical data

steps.description: The grain size data were analyzed using GRADISTAT.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze grain size data using GRADISTAT

steps.description: The bacterial 16S sequencing and metabolomics data were subjected to further statistical analysis using multivariate statistics.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform multivariate statistical analysis on bacterial 16S sequencing and metabolomics data

steps.description: The data were first imported, matched by sample location identifiers (metadata), and log-transformed to normalize the data using SIMCA 16.
steps.searchable_summary: Import, match by metadata, and log-transform data using SIMCA 16

steps.description: Partial Least Square-Discriminant Analysis (PLS-DA) was performed by finding successive orthogonal components from the estuary-specific datasets with maximum squared covariance.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform PLS-DA on estuary-specific datasets

steps.description: MetaboAnalyst 4.0 was used for differential metabolite abundances and pathway enrichment analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Use MetaboAnalyst 4.0 for metabolite abundance and pathway enrichment analysis

steps.description: Chemical clusters based on structural similarity were created for metabolic examination using the ChemRICH analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Create chemical clusters using ChemRICH analysis

steps.description: Automated taxonomic-to-phenotypic mapping was performed using Burrito and MIMOSA2.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform taxonomic-to-phenotypic mapping using Burrito and MIMOSA2

steps.description: Alpha-diversity indices such as Shannon-Weiner's H', Simpson's D1, Simpson's dominance D2, Simpson's evenness E, and Pielou's evenness J were used to calculate the taxonomic diversity and functional diversity within each community.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate alpha-diversity indices for taxonomic and functional diversity

steps.description: For diversity between communities, beta-diversity indices such as Jaccard's CJ and Sorensen's CS were calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate beta-diversity indices for community diversity

steps.description: For multiple group comparisons (≥ 3 groups), significant features were identified using one-way ANOVA statistical analyses (p < 0.05) using a posthoc Dunn's significance test.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify significant features using one-way ANOVA and Dunn's test

steps.description: Venn diagrams, presented in the supplementary section, were created using the InteractiVenn online web tool.
steps.searchable_summary: Create Venn diagrams using InteractiVenn

methods.description: This method involves the analysis of metabolomics data to identify and map metabolites, perform pathway enrichment analysis, and explore chemical clusters in estuarine systems.

steps.description: In total, 1681 discovery polar metabolite features were detected, of which 727 metabolite features were identified in the sediment samples collected from across four estuaries.
steps.searchable_summary: Detect and identify metabolite features in sediment samples

steps.description: The PCA model was found to be significant, with good linearity R2X (cum) = 0.639 and predictability Q2 (cum) = 0.484.
steps.searchable_summary: Evaluate PCA model significance and predictability

steps.description: To explore potential for metabolic pathway activity levels in each estuarine system, the metabolomic data were subjected to pathway enrichment analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform pathway enrichment analysis on metabolomic data

steps.description: Pathway impact measured the relative significance of all metabolites of a single pathway for the overall spectrum of metabolites.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure pathway impact for metabolite significance

steps.description: We then performed a ChemRICH class annotation of the KEGG identified metabolites and found that 70 chemical clusters were represented.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform ChemRICH class annotation for chemical clusters

methods.description: This method involves the analysis of bacterial communities in estuarine sediments to determine taxonomic diversity and community composition.

steps.description: Bacterial communities (with relative abundance ≥ 1%) from the four estuaries were taxonomically diverse, comprising 15 bacterial phyla.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze taxonomic diversity of bacterial communities

steps.description: Taxonomic profiling demonstrated that the most abundant phyla were Proteobacteria, Bacteroidota, Chloro exi, and Desulfobacterota.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform taxonomic profiling of bacterial phyla

steps.description: The presence of cyanobacteria, albeit in low relative abundances, could explain the higher chlorophyll-a content in these samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Correlate cyanobacteria presence with chlorophyll-a content
